title: Determination of optimal knot and bandwidth in nonparametric regression
author: Putra, Fadhlurrahman, et al.

steps.title: GWTSNR estimation procedures
steps.instruction: Next, we will determine the parameter estimates β˜(ui,vi) dan δ˜(ui,vi) from the GWTSNR model using Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE). The steps are as follows:a.Determine the probability density function of yi.(8)f(yi)=12πσ2exp(−12σ2[yi−(β0(ui,vi)+∑p=1l∑k=1mβpk(ui,vi)xpik+∑p=1l∑h=1rδp,m+h(ui,vi)(xpi−Kph)+m)]2)

steps.title: GWTSNR estimation procedures
steps.instruction: b. Establish a likelihood function.L(β˜(ui,vi),δ˜(ui,vi),σ2|Y˜)=∏i=1nf(yi|β˜(ui,vi),δ˜(ui,vi),σ2)(9)=(2π)−n2(σ2)−n2exp(−12σ2(Y˜−Xβ˜(ui,vi)−Pδ˜(ui,vi))′(Y˜−Xβ˜(ui,vi)−Pδ˜(ui,vi)))c.Establish a weighted likelihood function at the j-th location.f(yi)=12πσ2exp(−12σ2[yi−(β0(ui,vi)+∑p=1l∑k=1mβpk(ui,vi)xpik+∑p=1l∑h=1rδp,m+h(ui,vi)(xpi−Kph)+m)]2)(10)=(2π)−n2(σ2)−n2exp(−12σ2(uj,vj)∑i=1nwi(j)[yj−(β0(uj,vj)+∑p=1l∑k=1mβpk(uj,vj)xpik+∑p=1l∑h=1rδp,m+h(uj,vj)(xpi−Kph)+m)]2)

steps.title: GWTSNR estimation procedures
steps.instruction: wi(j): the weighting value at the i-th location to the j-location.d.Calculating ln from the weighted likelihood function.(11)lnL(β˜(uj,vj),δ˜(uj,vj),σ2|Y˜)=−n2(ln(2π)+σ2)−12σ2(Y˜−Xβ˜(uj,vj)−Pδ˜(uj,vj))′W(uj,vj)(Y˜−Xβ˜(uj,vj)−Pδ˜(uj,vj))where W(uj,vj)=diag(w1(uj,vj),w2(uj,vj),…,wn(uj,vj)).e.Maximize ln L

steps.title: GWTSNR estimation procedures
steps.instruction: Furthermore, to obtain the estimator of β˜^(uj,vj)and δ˜^(uj,vj) can be obtained by completing the following optimization:(13)maxβ˜,δ˜,σ2{lnL(β˜(uj,vj),δ˜(uj,vj),σ2|Y˜)}=maxβ˜,δ˜,σ2{−n2ln(2π)−n2ln(σ2)−12σ2Ψ}

steps.title: GWTSNR estimation procedures
steps.instruction: In other words, the estimators β˜^(ui,vi)and δ˜^(ui,vi) can be obtained with MLE by completing the following optimization:(14)maxβ˜,δ˜,σ2{lnL(β˜(ui,vi),δ˜(ui,vi),σ2|Y˜)}=maxβ˜,δ˜,σ2{−n2ln(2π)−n2ln(σ2)−12σ2Ψ}

steps.title: GWTSNR estimation procedures
steps.instruction: To obtain an estimator β˜^(ui,vi) that is independent on δ˜^(ui,vi), then substitute Eq. 16 into Eq. 15 as follows.β˜⌢(ui,vi)=(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)Y˜−(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)P(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)Y˜+(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)P(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)Xβ˜⌢(ui,vi)β˜^(ui,vi)−(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)P(P′W(uj,vj)P)−1P′W(uj,vj)Xβ˜^(ui,vi)=(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)Y˜−(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)P(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)Y˜[I−(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)P(P′W(uj,vj)P)−1P′W(uj,vj)X]β˜^(ui,vi)=(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)Y˜−(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)P(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)Y˜β˜^(ui,vi)=[I−(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)P(P′W(uj,vj)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)X]−1[(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)Y˜−(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)P(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)Y˜]β˜^(ui,vi)=S(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1[X′−X′W(ui,vi)P(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′]W(ui,vi)Y˜(17)β˜^(ui,vi)=A(K)Y˜Where,A(K)=S(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1[X′−X′W(ui,vi)P(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′]W(ui,vi)S=[I−(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)P(P′W(uj,vj)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)X]−1.

steps.title: GWTSNR estimation procedures
steps.instruction: To obtain an estimator δ˜^(ui,vi) that is independent on β˜^(ui,vi), then substitute Eq. 15 into Eq. 16 as follows.δ˜^(ui,vi)=(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)Y˜−(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)X((X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)Y˜)+(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)X((X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)Pδ˜^(ui,vi))δ˜^(ui,vi)−(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)X(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)Pδ˜^(ui,vi)=(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)Y˜−(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)X(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)Y˜δ˜^(ui,vi)=[I−(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)X(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)P]−1(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)Y˜−(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)X(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)Y˜δ˜^(ui,vi)=R[(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)Y˜−(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1P′W(ui,vi)X(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′W(ui,vi)Y˜]δ˜^(ui,vi)=R(P′W(ui,vi)P)−1[P′−P′W(ui,vi)X(X′W(ui,vi)X)−1X′]W(ui,vi)Y˜(18)δ˜^(ui,vi)=B(K)Y˜

steps.title: The research steps
steps.instruction: The steps of analysis in the research are as follows:a.Describe the morbidity rate in North Sumatra and its predictors.b.Make a scatterplot between the morbidity rate and each predictor to determine the relationship pattern.c.Do spatial heterogeneity tests using the Breusch-Pagan method.d.Calculates the Euclidean distance between the i-th location (ui,vi)and the j-th location (uj,vj)e.Determine the best weighting of the kernel functions, namely Gaussian, Bisquare, Tricube, and Exponential, based on the minimum GCV value.f.Choose the optimum knot point based on the minimum GCV value.g.Get the best GWTSNR model.h.Test the fit model hypothesis between the GWTSNR model and the TSNR model.i.Determine parameter significance tests simultaneously and partially.j.Interpret the GWTSNR model.k.Map 33 districts/cities in North Sumatra based on significant predictor variables.
